chunks.title: Catalog Description
chunks.content:
Special Topics in... Credit 0 to 4. Selected topics in an identified area of computer
science. May be repeated for credit. Prerequisite: Approval of instructor.

chunks.title: Course Learning Outcomes
chunks.content:
Upon completion of this course, the learner will be able to:
• Evaluate properties of real and synthetic networks using several C++ and
Python software packages
• Develop and evaluate graph machine learning models, including graph neural
networks and recommendation systems.
• Create knowledge graphs from unstructured data and perform graph
embedding for link predictions in knowledge graphs.
• Read research papers on graph machine learning and compare various state-
of-the-art methods

chunks.title: Textbook and/or Resource Materials
chunks.content:
This material Is: Optional
Graph Representation Learning
ISBN: 978-3-031-00460-5
Authors: William L. Hamilton

chunks.title: Grading Policy
chunks.content:
• Projects (proposal, presentation, and report): 30%
• Midterm exam: 30%
• Homework: 30%
• Paper Reading and Presentation: 10%
• The grading scale will be:
A. = 90-100
B. = 80-89
C. = 70-79
D. = 60-69 F = <60

chunks.title: Late Work Policy
chunks.content:
Work submitted by a student as makeup work for an excused absence is not
considered late work and is exempt from the late work policy (Student Rule 7).

chunks.title: Course Specific Late Work Policy
chunks.content:
Late submissions will incur a 20% penalty for each day past the deadline. Assignments submitted
more than 4 days late will receive no credit.

chunks.title: Course Schedule
chunks.content:
| Week | Topics |
|---|---|
| 1 | Overview of graph analysis, definitions, characterizing graphs; Analyzing characterizing practical graphs using Python and NetworkX |
| 2 | Introduction to random graph models; Compare random graphs with real graphs |
| 3 | Graph embedding models, such as deepwalk and node2vec; Implement graph embedding using random walks |
| 4 | Introduction to graph neural networks (GNNS) |
| 5 | Advanced graph neural network model |
| 6 | Interpretable graph neural networks; Scalable training methods for GNNS |
| 7 | Introduction to recommendation systems Midterm Exam |
| 8 | Introduction to knowledge graphs; Knowledge graph embedding |
| 9 | Knowledge graph representation learning |
| 10 | Introduction to graph databases |
| 11 | Integrating knowledge graphs with large language models |
| 12 | GraphRAG based LLM |
| 13 | Scientific applications with graph ML and knowledge graphs |
| 14 | Research paper reading |
| 15 | Project final presentation |
